chunks.title: for Loop with else in Python
chunks.text: In Python programming, we saw for loops.  But do you know that you can use else with for loop?  Today's video is about this.  Let's go to the computer screen and see how to use the else statement with for loop.  Let's get started.

chunks.title: for Loop with else in Python
chunks.text: In many interviews, people are trapped.  They are asked if else can be used with only if.  Can else be used with for or while loops?  The answer is yes.  So I want to show you a quick demo of this.

chunks.title: for Loop with else in Python
chunks.text: We can use else with for loop.  The simple thing is that if I write for i in range 5  and if I write print i after colon  and then I write else and I print  sorry no i and I run it.

chunks.title: for Loop with else in Python
chunks.text: If the control is not able to go inside the for loop,  then it will go inside the else.  This is the general thing.  And that's about it.  If we write i in range 5,

chunks.title: for Loop with else in Python
chunks.text: then it goes from 0 to 4.  You and everyone in the world know this.  But after 0, 1, 2, 3, 4,  the value of i cannot be 5  because we know that range works from 0 to n-1.

chunks.title: for Loop with else in Python
chunks.text: So in this case, else will be executed after that.  If I write here for i in an empty list,  I gave an empty list.  There is nothing in it.  So it is obvious that it will go inside else

chunks.text: and it will give me sorry no i.  So we can use else with for loop.  So here I have written this thing.  As you have learned before,  the else clause is used along with the if statement.

chunks.title: for Loop with else in Python
chunks.text: Else clause is used along with the if statement.  Python allows the else keyword to be used with the for loops  and while loops as well.  You can use the else keyword with while loops as well.  The else block appears after the body of the loop.

chunks.title: for Loop with else in Python
chunks.text: The statements in the else block will be executed  after all iterations are completed.  The program exits the loop only after the else block is executed.  That means if you are running a for loop or a while loop,  then at the end when all the values are finished,

chunks.title: for Loop with else in Python
chunks.text: then your else will be executed.  However, I will ask you a question.  This question is especially for those who think it is very easy.  If I write here if i equals to equals to 0 break,  you tell me,

chunks.text: I will do one thing, I will move the print i up a little.  If i equals to equals to, let's do one thing,  let's do 4 and I will arrange it again.  And I will arrange 6.  If I am breaking this for loop after 4,

If you think it is easy, then tell me in the comment section below.  Some of you must be thinking that it is very easy  that you can use else with for loop and else with while loop.

chunks.text: One more time, the loop did not break, the loop has ended.  It has ended successfully.  And its last value has reached there.  And now it has ended, the last value has been processed,  the last iteration has been processed.
